era: 1988-1993. Post-Hardcore/Rock. E=7 -- melodically ambitious, rhythmically complex, emotionally direct. Boise to Seattle. C/Z Records.
associated_musicians: Built to Spill (Doug Martsch's next band, 1992-present)

key_releases.title: Time Whore
key_releases.label: Silence Records
key_releases.significance: Debut. Boise underground. Martsch voice present.

key_releases.title: Guilt, Regret and Embarassment
key_releases.label: C/Z Records
key_releases.significance: C/Z debut. Pacific Northwest profile established. Fully developed melodic approach.

key_releases.title: Just Kidding
key_releases.label: C/Z Records
key_releases.significance: Final record. Transition to Built to Spill. Dissolution document.

theory_nodes.concept: BOISE ENTERS THE ENGINE. First Idaho entry in the engine. Boise-Seattle creative corridor: 400 miles southeast, I-84, both communities active. Pacific Northwest music network is not limited to Washington state. The corridor sustains independent music at the Boise-Seattle scale.
theory_nodes.description: First Idaho entry. Boise-Seattle creative corridor established in engine.

theory_nodes.concept: Doug Martsch pre-Built to Spill. Martsch's melodic guitar sensibility -- unexpected intervals, unlikely note choices, resolution that was not predictable -- is fully present in Treepeople (1988-1993) before Built to Spill formed (1992-1993). Treepeople is the lab; Built to Spill is the result. Listeners of BtS's major-label work (Perfect From Now On 1997, Keep It Like a Secret 1999) are hearing the downstream of what Treepeople established.
theory_nodes.description: Treepeople as the developmental context for Doug Martsch's guitar voice.

theory_nodes.concept: C/Z Records and the Deep Six compilation (1986). C/Z released Deep Six -- the root document of the grunge lineage: Melvins, U-Men, Soundgarden, Green River, Skin Yard, Malfunkshun. The label was present at the origin of the Seattle underground. Treepeople (263) and Skin Yard (264) are both C/Z bands -- label continuity across these two degrees. C/Z as alternative infrastructure to Sub Pop.
theory_nodes.description: C/Z Records: Deep Six 1986 and the grunge root document.

theory_nodes.concept: E=7 three-mechanism streak (261-263). Screaming Trees (261): psychedelic density. Gas Huffer (262): speed and volume. Treepeople (263): post-hardcore melodic and rhythmic complexity. Three consecutive E=7 readings achieved by three entirely different acoustic mechanisms. The E-scale measures presence and impact, reachable by multiple routes.
theory_nodes.description: E=7 streak: three degrees, three mechanisms, same energy.

cross_references.relationship: E=7 continues. Garage Rock speed to Post-Hardcore melodic complexity. Fremont to Boise-Seattle.
C/Z Records labelmate. Both on C/Z. Deep Six compilation connection. Label continuity across 263-264.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=7 (Treepeople) + E=5 (P-wave). Both travel through structure -- Martsch's guitar lines travel through complex harmonic structures; P-waves travel through rock strata. The energy propagates through the medium.

carry_forward: Built to Spill (Doug Martsch) will enter the engine in future degrees. Treepeople (263) is the root context.
